summary: A course syllabus for Pieter Abbeel's Fall 2015 CS287 Advanced Robotics class at UC Berkeley, outlining his pedagogical focus on probabilistic reasoning and optimization as the mathematical foundations of modern robotics.

principles.statement: State-of-the-art robotic systems are fundamentally built on probabilistic reasoning and optimization.
principles.rationale: These mathematical areas form the core algorithms of modern robotics and have broad applicability across all of Artificial Intelligence.
principles.source_quote: The majority of these techniques are heavily based on probabilistic reasoning and optimization---two areas with wide applicability in modern Artificial Intelligence.

heuristics: When writing a research paper, focus on the problem setting, novelty, approach, and results; only survey prior work that is directly relevant to understanding your approach.

signature_quotes.text: The majority of these techniques are heavily based on probabilistic reasoning and optimization---two areas with wide applicability in modern Artificial Intelligence.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the core mathematical foundations of advanced robotics.

anti_patterns.description: Rewriting or over-explaining prior work in a research paper when it isn't directly relevant to the new approach.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It distracts from the core focus of the paper, which should be the problem setting, novelty, approach, results, and limitations.
